gap.description: Deviation between C566 calibrated reading and Creator ground truth

hypotheses.H1_linear_gain: DHT22 has non-unity gain (underdetermined with 1 GT point)
hypotheses.H2_nonlinearity: DHT22 error varies with humidity level (underdetermined)

conclusion: Two equally plausible explanations — environmental change vs Kalman overestimation. Second GT measurement needed to resolve.

forward_prediction.description: The 9.43% humidity gap between C566 calibrated reading and Creator GT is due to EITHER environmental change (true humidity dropped from 61% to ~52%) OR Kalman bias overestimation. Resolvable with second ground-truth measurement.

prediction.if_52_to_55: bias IS 45.93%, environment changed — C566 calibration was correct
prediction.if_58_to_61: bias is ~36%, Kalman overestimated — need recalibration

forward_prediction.grading_criteria: CORRECT if Creator's next measurement resolves which hypothesis is true (both outcomes are accounted for), INCORRECT if measurement is outside 45-65% range (would indicate third explanation)
